title: Trump’s AI Action Plan removes ‘red tape’ for AI developers and data centers, punishes states that act alone
summary: David Sacks, U.S. President Donald Trump's "AI and Crypto Czar", speaks to President Trump as he signs a series of executive orders in the Oval Office of the White House on Jan. 23, 2025 in Washington, D.C. Trump signed a range of executive orders pertaining to issues including crypto currency and artificial intelligence. (Photo by Anna Moneymaker/Getty Images) The Trump administration wants to greatly expand the development and use of advanced artificial intelligence, including rolling back environmental rules to spur building of power-thirsty data centers and punishing states that attempt to regulate AI on their own . The administration’s action plan, called “ Winni...

title: AST Escape: Fairbanks [AK25072838]
summary: On 7/23/2025 at approximately 1555 hours, Fairbanks AST received a report from 3022 Parks Highway that a male, Ethan Wiehl-Ortiz, had escaped from the North Star Center. An investigation is ongoing.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: Burn Permit Suspensions Lifted for Tok
summary: Tok Burn Suspension Lifted, Permits Now Available Statewide With cooler weather and widespread rains returning to Interior Alaska, the Alaska Division of Forestry & Fire Protection has announced the lifting of the burn suspension for the Tok area. This makes Tok the last area in the state to have it...

title: Crews depart Tlozhavun Fire after hard work yields 100% containment and complete extinguishment
summary: Firefighters on the Tlozhavun Fire have completely controlled and suppressed the 22-acre fire 10 miles south of Fort Yukon and will transition off the incident to support other firefighting efforts. Steady progress on Ptarmigan Complex fires continued Tuesday, including the removal of structure protection equipment because fire is no longer posing a threat to th...

title: Wyoming Type 3 IMT to transfer command of the Goldrun Complex
summary: The Wyoming Type 3 Incident Management Team 1 will turn over command of the Goldrun Complex to a local Type 4 organization at 7a.m., Friday, July 25, 2025 The Wyoming Team 1 is grateful to the community of Ambler for all the support it has received while assisting in the management of the complex’s...

title: AST Found explosives: Glennallen [AK25069026]
summary: On 07/23/2025 at approximately 1100 hours, the Alaska State Troopers along with FBI EOD and military teams responded to the area of mile 115 Richardson Highway for a report of found explosives. The explosives were located and disposed of by EOD personnel. The Richardson Highway was closed periodical...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: All evacuation orders and temporary flight restrictions have been lifted off the Bear Creek and the Saint George Creek Fires
summary: Backhaul coming off the Bear Creek Fire is loaded on to a stake bed truck to be returned to Palmer for processing. Key Message: Evacuations on the Saint George Fire are lifted. The Bear Creek Fire has also had all evacuation orders, and its Temporary Flight Restriction (TFR) removed. Bear Creek Fire...

title: Rain and reduced temperatures continue to suppress fire activity on the Himalaya Road group fires
summary: Burned trees and ground on the Aggie Creek Fire. Area Map for Himalaya Road, Aggie Creek, and Obrien Fires for July 23, 2025. title: Transition underway as new Incident Managment Team takes command on the Nenana Ridge Complex

title: Wednesday, July 23, 2025
summary: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney recently held a summit with some Indigenous leaders to address the issue of laws that allow the fast tracking of major development projects. As Dan Karpenchuk reports, many of those leaders say they’re still critical of the new laws, while others are taking more of a wait-and-see attitude. Carney was trying to ease First Nations fears about the major projects law, but man...

summary: On July 8, 2025 Kiril Martushev age 37 from Homer was cited by the Alaska Wildlife Troopers during the Bristol Bay Salmon Enforcement Program for commercial fishing during a closed period in Nushigak. Arraignment is scheduled in Dillingham District Court on August 5, 2025.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: Bits of fishing gear and buoys dominate marine debris load in Alaska, report says
summary: U.S. Coast Guard Chief Petty Officer Jeremiah Moon ties a trash bag full of garbage collected from Kodiak's Jewel Beach on May 6, 2011. Coast Guard base Kodiak holds an annual beach cleanup that is timed near Earth Day. Marine debris that collects on Alaska beaches is dominated by bits of fishing nets, fishing line and other fishing gear, a new report says. (Photo by Petty Officer 3rd Class Charly Hengen/U.S. Coast Guard) Marine debris that pollutes Alaska’s expansive coastlines is dominated by discarded fishing m...

title: AST Sport Fishing Violation: Soldotna [AK25072343]
summary: On 7/22/2025 Alaska Wildlife Troopers contacted Michael Kim, age 58 of Washington State. Kim was observed sport fishing at Soldotna Creek Park. Kim Failed to Release Snagged Fish while fishing. Kim was issued a citation with a fine of $140.

title: AST Probation Violation: Wasilla [AK25072576]
summary: UPDATE: After Kerr was remanded a user amount of methamphetamine was found hidden on his person. Kerr is now being charged with the additional charges of Promoting Contraband 1st (Class C Felony) and Misconduct Involving a Controlled Substance (Class C Felony). On 07/23/2025 at approximately 0921 hours, the Highway Interdiction K9 Team conducted a traffic stop on a gray Dodge Charger for a moving violation and expired registration on the Parks Highway near Palmer Wasilla Highway. Investigation revealed the passenger, Ronald Kerr, age 29 of Wasilla, had violated his probation. Kerr was arrested and remanded to MAT-SU Pretrial on a Probation Violation.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

charges: the additional charges of Promoting Contraband 1st (Class C Felony)
Misconduct Involving a Controlled Substance (Class C Felony)

title: Former state legislator wins lawsuit that sought to enforce a clause of the Alaska Constitution
summary: A copy of the Alaska Constitution is seen on Thursday, July 28, 2022. (Photo by James Brooks/Alaska Beacon) Former state Rep. David Eastman has won his lawsuit against Gov. Mike Dunleavy and the state of Alaska, successfully challenging the governor’s decision to allow a bill to become law last year...

title: PBS and NPR are generally unbiased and independent of government propaganda
summary: A host and guest in July 2024 sit inside a recording studio at KMXT, the public radio station on Kodiak Island in Alaska. (Photo by Nathaniel Herz/Northern Journal) Champions of the almost entirely party-line vote in the U.S. Senate to erase $1.1 billion in already approved funds for the Corporation for Public Broadcasting called their action a refusal to subsidize liberal media. “Public broadcasting has long been overtaken by partisan activists,” said U.S. Sen. Ted Cruz of Texas, insisting there is no need for government to fund what he regards as biased media. “If you want to watch the left-wing propaganda, turn on MSNBC,” Cruz said. Accusing the media of liberal bias has been a consistent conservative complaint since the civil rights era, when white Southerners insisted news outlets we...

title: Organ donation groups seek to reassure US House panel after ‘deeply concerning’ report
summary: A U.S. House subcommittee heard testimony on organ donation Tuesday following a scathing report from the Health Resources and Services Administration. (Photo: Getty Images) WASHINGTON — The heads of several organ transplant organizations testified before Congress on Tuesday that they are working to earn back the public’s trust following the release of a scathing report. In a rare example of bipartisanship on Capitol Hill, both Republicans and Democrats on a U.S. House Energy and Commerce subcommittee expressed concern with several cases and interest in enhanced oversight of the entities that manage organ donation. The central issue, according to testimony from witnesses and comments from lawmakers, is that organ donations increasingly come from people experiencing circulatory death as opposed to brain death. Dr. Raymond Lynch, chief of the Organ Transplant Bran...

title: Wrangell’s water remains stable, but boil water notice still in effect
summary: Wrangell’s new water treatment plant is almost ready to serve the community. It’s in its commissioning stage on Feb. 13, where they ensure everything is properly working. (Colette Czarnecki/KSTK) Tuesday was Wrangell’s third day with a boil water notice in effect, though the water quality was improving....
